channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Graffiti on rear of property, 13th March
item.description:
There’s graffiti on the rear of one of the houses opposite the way - they may not be aware but they’re on the other side of the railway and not known to us.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Northumberland Avenue, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog mess black bags by 97and 95 Lyndon Avenue dogs mess, 13th March
item.description:
There are many dogs' mess bags along Lyndon Avenue that need clearing asap. This was reported last week and it has still not been cleared but no reference number.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 97, Lyndon Avenue, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 29 Brondesbury villas, 13th March
item.description:
Rubbish on tree verge

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Villas, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Weston house Winchester sve, 13th March
item.description:
This are is becoming a dumping ground and what happens is one person puts something there and more then dump there. There should be a sign saying don’t do it.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Winchester Avenue, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load (Estates)

item.title: Waste Bins and Rats Near Residential Entrance, 13th March
item.description:
Am writing to make a complaint regarding a serious issue outside our residential building. Next to the entrance gate of our residential flats, there is a pub that keeps its rubbish and food waste bins directly beside the entrance used by residents. Unfortunately, these bins are often not placed properly and are frequently left overflowing or positioned in a way that blocks or restricts access to the gate. Because of the large amount of food waste and the way the bins are left outside, there has been a growing problem with rats around the entrance area. This creates an unpleasant environment and raises serious concerns about hygiene, health, and safety for residents who need to enter and leave the building every day. We would like the council to investigate this issue and ensure that the pub manages its waste properly. The bins should be stored in an appropriate place and handled in a way that prevents vermin and keeps the entrance clear for residents. I would appreciate it if this matter could be looked into as soon as possible. Thank you for your attention.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Walters Yard, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken playground equipment, 13th March
item.description:
The zip line in the childrens playground in ladywell park is broken and cannot be used

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Malyons Road, Ladywell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bike, 13th March
item.description:
This bike has been here for years and the tyres have gone flat during this time. Believe it used to belong to the homeless guy who used to live in old farm park and had bikes all over the borough. This hasn't moved in years

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 111, Station Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Speed limit sign knocked down, 13th March
item.description:
The 20mph speed limit sign has been knocked down.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 108, Alleyn Road, Dulwich, Greater London

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 12, Red Post Hill, Dulwich Village, Greater London

item.title: Graffiti and flyposting needs to be removed please, 13th March
item.description:
.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Court Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog fouling, 13th March
item.description:
I have already reported that there is a serious issue on this street but the council marks it as "Solved". This is shameful because you are not doing anything and this has to be fixed because we've already endured over a year of dog poo on this street. The council just got tens of millions in funding but then ignores an issue like this? Of course, it's not in a "nice street" next to council estates so who cares?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Floyd Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: I will keep on reporting this until its solves, 13th March
item.description:
I have already reported that there is a serious issue on this street but the council marks it as "Solved". Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lansdowne Mews, Charlton

item.title: Extensive broken slabs. Trip hazards, 13th March
item.description:
Extensive broken slabs outside 33/35/37 shuttlemead. Driveway ramps too high and damages cars. Over 30ml raised trip hazards. Otherside of road pavement was resurfaced years ago and our side left to crumble.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shuttlemead, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No fishing sign in danson park, 13th March
item.description:
The no fishing sign in danson park by the lake has been removed and there is around 7 youths fishing there right now. please can this be looked into urgently.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Danson Lane, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lifted paving very dangerous, 13th March
item.description:
The paving slab has lifted causing severe trip hazard

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mainridge Road, Chislehurst
